explanation: Policy Management Backend shows clear signs of Microservice Greedy: it receives HTTP connections from Customer Self-Service Frontend, Policy Management Frontend, and Customer Management Backend; communicates via ActiveMQ with both Customer Self-Service Backend and Risk Management Server; and sends HTTP calls to both Customer Core and Spring Boot Admin. This makes it a central hub with too many inbound and outbound dependencies, acting as a bottleneck. Customer Core is also suspect as it receives HTTP calls from all three backend services (Customer Management Backend, Customer Self-Service Backend, and Policy Management Backend), suggesting it has accumulated too many data responsibilities that multiple services depend on, resembling a shared database anti-pattern wrapped in a service, violating single responsibility and creating a scalability bottleneck.
